subject: Lessons on gun violence from New York City (video)
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY DE BLASIO 2020
body:
The threat from domestic terrorism is unmistakable. This is the threat we need to focus on.
# [](https://act.billdeblasio.com/page/m/6bf21039/1630b3eb/511a4896/5f554f95/1153401259/VEsH/?g=VmvkeFNw52hdLWhQmfUOXQ "https://www.facebook.com/bill.deblasio/posts/2377005395726729")
Friend --
The rise in mass shootings across this country is part of an epidemic with a clear source. It's the proliferation of weapons and a movement of white supremacy aided and abetted by the White House. No other nation in the world comes close to experiencing the rate of mass shootings that we see across this country, and that's a fact.
**[_Watch Bill de Blasio address the causes of recent gun violence and what America can do to reduce gun violence the way he has in New York City:_](https://act.billdeblasio.com/page/m/6bf21039/1630b3eb/511a4896/5f554f95/1153401259/VEsE/?g=VmvkeFNw52hdLWhQmfUOXQ "https://www.facebook.com/bill.deblasio/posts/2377005395726729")**
[](https://act.billdeblasio.com/page/m/6bf21039/1630b3eb/511a4896/5f554f95/1153401259/VEsF/?g=VmvkeFNw52hdLWhQmfUOXQ "https://www.facebook.com/bill.deblasio/posts/2377005395726729")
We have to understand something has changed and we have to confront it. Bill is calling on Congress to come back from its recess right this minute and pass the basics to address this issue: universal background checks, a ban on assault weapons and waiting periods for gun purchases. That's what the American majority wants.
And Bill has seen it work. In New York City -- a place with some of the toughest gun laws in the country -- police are working more closely with communities to stop violence before it starts. The result: New York City is now the safest big city in America.
**[_Check out Bill 's remarks on gun violence now and share them with your friends and family. We need to do something about this._](https://act.billdeblasio.com/page/m/6bf21039/1630b3eb/511a4896/5f554f95/1153401259/VEsC/?g=VmvkeFNw52hdLWhQmfUOXQ "https://www.facebook.com/bill.deblasio/posts/2377005395726729")**
Bill has proof that strong gun safety laws correlate to reduced gun violence, and we've all got proof that Donald Trump has normalized this kind of hate and given it permission. Now we've got to stop it.
Thanks for watching,
Team de Blasio

[WATCH»](https://act.billdeblasio.com/page/m/6bf21039/1630b3eb/511a4896/5f554f95/1153401259/VEsD/?g=VmvkeFNw52hdLWhQmfUOXQ "https://www.facebook.com/bill.deblasio/posts/2377005395726729")

Team,
My name is Maddie Collins. I fled Liberia in 1990 during the bloody civil war that killed 250,000 Liberians. I barely escaped. It was with my husband, Wilmot that we escaped starvation and death.
I'm proud to stand beside my husband, my best friend and my life partner today and every day.
[**Show Wilmot you support him by donating $5 today.**](https://act.wilmotcollins.?g=ENsuPVNw52hc6i6rfY8XtkA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wc-email?refcode=em190806&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=")
When Wilmot and I were separated for nearly 3 years, I always fought for him as he fought to get back to me.
His fight to reunite our family, torn apart by civil war, is the same way he will fight for Montana, always.
Wilmot is a fighter for those who need a hand up. He's fought for others his whole career. Now, we're in for the fight of our lives to save America, restore the promise of this country, and preserve our planet. We have no time to waste getting started.
[**Your $5 contribution is a powerful way to invest in Wilmot's candidacy and show you're part of this movement. clean_body: "" President Trump is openly stoking racism and hatred to advance an authoritarian agenda, and Republicans nationwide are marching in lockstep. The only way to truly fight back is to hold competitive primaries where we can elect Democrats who will best represent the popular progressive policies that voters want to support in the general election. Unfortunately, the DCCC continues to actively hurt Democrats and help Donald Trump by "blacklisting" anyone who works with candidates who are outside of the Democratic establishment. Here's the thing: Our Revolution refuses to back down. We are going to keep supporting diverse and progressive Congresswomen like Ilhan Omar (MN), Rashida Tlaib (MI), and Deb Haaland (NM), and continue to upend traditional notions about candidate electability in swing states across our country's heartland. We are going to keep shattering the establishment's myth of invulnerable incumbency as we did by supporting Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez and Ayanna Pressley. "" "" "" "" "" "" ""** The DCCC has a responsibility to stop actively hurting candidates with the best chance to win in November, and last week's mass resignations due to their failure to respect diversity are only the latest example of their stunning naivety about what it takes to win in the Trump era. We are going to keep fighting -- with, or without them. "" Onward, The whole team at Our Revolution PAID FOR BY OUR REVOLUTION PO Box 66208 WASHINGTON, DC, 20035 Not authorized by any candidate or candidate committee. name: Tina Smith
subject: Sign now: become a citizen co-sponsor of the Democracy for All amendment to overturn Citizens United
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tina Smith for Minnesota
body:
The Citizens United decision unleashed a flood of unlimited corporate spending into our elections.

| [](https://action.tinaforminnesota.?g=YCjTcDpTVzRarKyUt8_RZpA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tsm-email-aug2019?refcode=em190807citizensutd-full&recurring=auto&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=") Friend

The Citizens United decision unleashed a flood of unlimited corporate spending into our elections. Giving those with the most power -- corporations, special interests, and the wealthy -- _even more_ influence in our political system.

That's why I'm standing with Democrats in the Senate to fight back. **We just introduced a constitutional amendment to take back our democracy and overturn Citizens United. I am looking for 5,000 grassroots activists to become citizen co-sponsors of our amendment. Can you be one of them?**

**[Add your name here as a citizen co-sponsor of our Democracy for All amendment to overturn Citizens United. It's important to have as many people as possible on the record with us. Only a large number will pressure Republicans to take action on this.](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc4eea7/c1f7015/64e0f851/1146f694/2211713487/VEsE/?g=YCjTcDpTVzRarKyUt8_RZpA "https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/s/democracy-for-all-amendment?source=em190806citizensutd-c&recurring=auto")** **[SIGN THE PETITION](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc4eea7/c1f7015/64e0f851/1146f694/2211713487/VEsF/?g=YCjTcDpTVzRarKyUt8_RZpA "https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/s/democracy-for-all-amendment?source=em190806citizensutd-c&recurring=auto")** Elections shouldn't be decided by who can write the biggest check or who has the best lawyers and can manipulate the laws to their advantage. They _should_ be decided by who has the best ideas -- and who can organize people behind them.

That is why I am fighting for the _Democracy for All Amendment_ to overturn the unfair court ruling in Citizens United and allow for Congress to set reasonable rules around corporate and special interest spending in elections.

**[Will you add your name as citizen co-sponsor here and be part of the movement that puts the power back in the hands of everyday people? We cannot win this fight without everyone on this team.](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc4eea7/c1f7015/64e0f851/1146f694/2211713487/VEsC/?g=YCjTcDpTVzRarKyUt8_RZpA "https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/s/democracy-for-all-amendment?source=em190806citizensutd-c&recurring=auto")**

Tina | **U.S. Senator Tina Smith is running for re-election in 2020 in a race _NBC News_ has already labeled a "race to watch" with Donald Trump targeting Minnesota for victory. Make sure she has the resources to fight back.** [CONTRIBUTE](https://action.tinaforminnesota.?g=YCjTcDpTVzRarKyUt8_RZpA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tsm-email-aug2019?refcode=em190807citizensutd-full-biobox&amount=10&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=&recurring=auto")

name: =?utf-8?Q?Juli=C3=A1n_Castro?=
subject: I'm heartbroken. I'm furious.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Julian for the Future
body:
I'm asking for your immediate signature. | | |
---
| |
---
| | [_**I 'm heartbroken. I'm furious. And I'm asking for your immediate signature. Americans shouldn't die while common sense gun legislation sits on Mitch McConnell's desk. Sign your name next to mine and demand he act NOW by cutting the Senate's vacation short. Senators must return to Washington to pass this urgent legislation. >> **_](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fcedc/3eb3e2fc/5cf9bf12/744bc0bd/519076951/VEsH/?g=5-wKdTjfd5lcsCTt4S4B-7A "https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/s/gun-violence-survey?source=20190807_em_pet_b1_castro_all") I'm sick to my stomach. A gunman opened fire on my community this weekend. I realized that my family's lives could be put in danger while we go back-to-school shopping. I'm heartbroken that this weekend -- in El Paso, and in Dayton, and in Chicago -- this was the reality for so many families. And I'm heartbroken that, in too many communities, this is the reality every day. **But I 'm also pissed off.** Because while Donald Trump fans the flames of hate and division... While white nationalism festers like a toxic brew across this country... While Americans are dying from preventable gun violence... Common sense gun reform that could have stopped it is sitting on Mitch McConnell's desk. [_**I 'm calling for a massive surge of support -- 50,000 signatures before midnight -- to demand McConnell cut the Senate's vacation short and pass this critical gun violence legislation immediately. Can I count on your signature? >>**_](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fcedc/3eb3e2fc/5cf9bf12/744bc0bd/519076951/VEsE/?g=5-wKdTjfd5lcsCTt4S4B-7A "https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/s/gun-violence-survey?source=20190807_em_pet_b1_castro_all") We are fighting both a serious gun violence epidemic, as well as a rise of white nationalism. Thoughts and prayers are the only solutions Republicans have to offer. But I'm completely out of thoughts and prayers. It's now up to us to rise up and address both of these crises with the urgency this moment demands. _**[Will you sign your name next to mine and help me reach 50,000 signatures strong before midnight? Let 's show the power of action behind our thoughts and prayers >>](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fcedc/3eb3e2fc/5cf9bf12/744bc0bd/519076951/VEsF/?g=5-wKdTjfd5lcsCTt4S4B-7A "https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/s/gun-violence-survey?source=20190807_em_pet_b1_castro_all")**_ Thank you, Julian
---

---
| | [](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fcedc/3eb3e2fc/5cf9bf12/744bc0ba/519076951/VEsA/?g=5-wKdTjfd5lcsCTt4S4B-7A "https://www.instagram.com/juliancastrotx/")

No one like Julian Castro has ever been in the Oval Office. His grandmother immigrated here from Mexico when she was just 7 years old. She never got past the 4th grade, but she provided for her family by working as a maid and a housekeeper.

Now, her grandson is running for President of the United States. Against all odds, grassroots Democrats like YOU helped him qualify for the Democratic debates and sent him to the front of the pack of Democratic candidates.

We know we send you a lot of emails, but that's because we're relying on grassroots supporters to overcome our next big challenge:

Julian must be able to compete and build a movement that lasts for the long haul, and he's not taking a cent of corporate PAC money to do it. Will you be a part of this historic campaign?

**[DONATE](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fcedc/3eb3e2fc/5cf9bf12/744bc0b9/519076951/VEsB/?g=5-wKdTjfd5lcsCTt4S4B-7A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/julian-em?refcode=email_footer_donate&amount=25&recurring=on&recur_weekly=on")**
Again, we know we send you a lot of emails. We hope you'll stick with us long-term. If you want to stay updated but receive fewer emails, please let us know [here](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fcedc/3eb3e2fc/5cf9bf12/744bc0b8/519076951/VEsO/?g=5-wKdTjfd5lcsCTt4S4B-7A "https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/s/receive-fewer-emails"). If you'd like to unsubscribe completely, we understand. Please unsubscribe [here](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fcedc/3eb3e2fc/5cf9bf12/744bc0a7/519076951/VEsP/?g=5-wKdTjfd5lcsCTt4S4B-7A "https://action.julianforthefuture.com/unsubscribe"), and we'll make sure to process your request.

Every time we receive a contribution from a grassroots donor, we're so excited we could frame it and put it up on the wall.

So we thought: why not do that with all of our donations?

Our donors are really important to everything we do.

It's thanks to our donors that we're able to run a different kind of campaign -- with no help from federal lobbyists or PACs of any kind. With no closed-door fundraisers.

It's thanks to our donors that Elizabeth is able to spend less time on fundraising and more time fighting for the issues that really matter.

So we're covering our walls here at HQ with the names of everyone who's contributed to own a piece of this campaign.

name: Cory Booker
subject: To love our country in this moment means we must step outside our comfort zones and confront ourselves. We need to ask hard questions and genuinely seek answers.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cory 2020
body:
Moments ago, in the wake of a weekend in which we witnessed violent acts of terror in America, I spoke at Emanuel AME Church in Charleston, SC about gun violence and the rising tide of hatred and white nationalism in America.
**You can watch what I had to say here:[https://youtu.be/mfkRcqbnZuc?t=257](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc08e7/5fca0d19/e9156fc/2918edaf/2565503346/VEsH/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://youtu.be/mfkRcqbnZuc?t=257")**
I was there today because of love. The kind of love I learned about in church growing up. The kind of courageous love of people who could love those who hated them, despised them, and cursed them.
**A heroic love that pushed people to march knowing they could be beaten, to board buses knowing they could be bombed.**
A love fueled by multi-racial, multi-ethnic coalitions that share a commitment to a common cause and to our common destiny. A radical love that rejects the sinister and dangerous delusion of otherness -- a delusion that divides, that weakens, that pits American against American to our own collective peril.
It requires us to admit when we are wrong, to be vulnerable about our mistakes and our contradictions, and to be willing to question what we sometimes hold sacred. This is a lesson I have learned over and over again in my own life.
And to love our country in this moment means we must step outside our comfort zones and confront ourselves. We need to ask hard questions and genuinely seek answers.
And that means we need to acknowledge that the very founding of our country was an act of profound contradiction.
Bigotry was written into our founding documents. Native Americans were referred to as "savages" and Black people as fractions of human beings. White supremacy has always been a problem in our American story -- if not always at the surface, then lurking not so far beneath it.
We have seen it from slavemasters who stole and pillaged Black bodies for profit to demagogues who stoked racist and anti-immigrant hatred for votes, then enshrined their bigotry into laws.
And, yes, racist violence has always been part of the American story -- never more so than in times of transition and rapid social change.
We have seen it from the Civil War to the Civil Rights Movement; from the Red Summer 100 years ago to Charlottesville; from the lynching of Latinx people in Porvenir, Texas 101 years ago to the massacre in El Paso, Texas this past Saturday.
To say this is to speak the truth plainly -- because without truth there is no reconciliation.
We must acknowledge as a country that as much as white supremacy manifests itself in dangerous and deadly acts of terror, it is perpetuated by what is too often a willful ignorance of its presence in our society.
It manifests itself in a criminal justice system that arrests Blacks at three times the rate of Whites for drug-related crimes despite virtually no difference between Whites and Blacks in the frequency of dealing or using drugs.
In an immigration system that targets Latinx migrants fleeing violence at our southern border, separates families and throws children in cages.
In a health care system that fails Black and Brown Americans, that dismisses the pain of Black women with deadly consequences and where undocumented people are afraid to seek care after a mass shooting.
**We must change our laws, but we must also confront our past. People 's very lives are in the balance. And to be frank, the future of the country hangs in the balance.**
**We have the power to act.** And we can act to legislate safety even if we cannot legislate love.
**We must act** to prevent people who should not have guns from getting them.
**We must act** to get weapons of war off our streets, out of our grocery stores, our bars, our temples, and our churches by banning assault weapons once and for all.
**We must require federal licensing for all guns in America -- a policy that we know will save lives.**
I went to church today to ask if we have the collective resolve to change the reality we live in, to ask if we have it in us to tell ourselves the honest story. Because we know the truth will set us free.
We know these are not problems that will go away inevitably. This is hard and painful and it will take hard work and sacrifice.
We must stand together and work together and struggle together for a new American freedom in our generation.
Freedom from fear, freedom from violence, freedom from hatred. Freedom to seek. Freedom to achieve. Freedom to dream America anew again.
Once and for all, let us be the land of the free.
There is only one way to get there -- and that is together.
And there is only one way to win -- with the power, healing, and the salvation we find in love.
Together,
Cory
[**_Click here to watch the full remarks at Emanuel AME Church._**](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc08e7/5fca0d19/e9156fc/2918edaf/2565503346/VEsE/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://youtu.be/mfkRcqbnZuc?t=257")

name: Abigail Spanberger
subject: I got the card!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Spanberger for Congress
body:
Peter,

Earlier today, Adam and the girls gave me a card with the well wishes he collected from people all over Virginia and the country, plus a few VA07 residents living overseas! I'm so grateful for the heartfelt birthday wishes and the math tutorial from one friend who noted that I'm "halfway to 80"! ;-)

On my birthday, I'm reflecting on all the people who have influenced me throughout my life, those who believed in me enough to elect me to Congress, and those whom I'm so honored to represent.

I find my daily motivation in the people whose stories drive me, whose strength inspires me, and whose belief in our communities and our country make them believe that we can always be better and do better and that progress is worth working and fighting for.

I'm working hard on Capitol Hill and back home in Virginia for those I represent and want to continue doing so. That means I'll need to get through a tough re-election campaign. name: Mike Thompson
subject: enough is enough
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Mike Thompson For Congress
body:
In the past week, communities in El Paso, Dayton, and Gilroy here in California have been shaken by more senseless gun violence. Yet again, words cannot do justice to the grief our country feels. Enough is enough.

The House passed my bipartisan bill to require background checks on all firearm purchases nearly 6 months ago--yet Mitch McConnell still refuses to bring it to a vote in the Senate.

**Lives are at stake every day McConnell fails to act. We need a solution immediately.[Tell Mitch McConnell to do his job and allow a vote on bills the House has already passed to help reduce gun violence.](https://act.myngp.com/el/R0xL8wmny6i-K4iHAzTz9-NHTDgHpcCj35-HN0PXDfI=/U9YjdmV7v7bqOybcXepm2M8syr9g-v3VNB190_oSOD8=)**

[](https://act.myngp.com/el/R0xL8wmny6i-K4iHAzTz9-NHTDgHpcCj35-HN0PXDfI=/U9YjdmV7v7bqOybcXepm2M8syr9g-v3VNB190_oSOD8=)
As communities across the country are in mourning over the loss of their loved ones, we are honoring their memories by demanding Senate action to prevent more tragedies like these.

It is far past time to bring relief to those affected by gun violence every day--and Mitch McConnell is the one person standing in the way of this urgent issue.

**[He'd rather call himself the Grim Reaper than pass a bill that would save lives. Add your name and demand Senate action to prevent gun violence.](https://act.myngp.com/el/R0xL8wmny6i-K4iHAzTz9-NHTDgHpcCj35-HN0PXDfI=/U9YjdmV7v7bqOybcXepm2M8syr9g-v3VNB190_oSOD8=) **

Thanks for making your voice heard.
Mike

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: President Trump and First Lady visit El Paso, Dayton today
body:
"Counselor to the President Kellyanne Conway said Tuesday morning that the president planned to meet with a number of people impacted by..." | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=c36eac65eb&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **President Trump and First Lady visit El Paso, Dayton today**

President Donald J. Trump and First Lady Melania Trump are visiting “El Paso, Texas, and Dayton, Ohio, on Wednesday in the wake of two mass shootings in the cities last weekend,” Nikki Schwab reports for the _New York Post_.

“Counselor to the President Kellyanne Conway said Tuesday morning that the president planned to meet with a number of people impacted by the dual tragedies.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=0b26a9bccd&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “The Trump administration has implemented an unprecedented pressure campaign on Iran’s leaders with two objectives: First, to deprive the Iranian regime of the money it needs to support its destabilizing activities. Second, to force the Ayatollah Ali Khamenei to the negotiating table to conclude a comprehensive and enduring deal,” Secretary of State Mike Pompeo writes in _[USA Today](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=c31b8738b2&e=3ee4985eea)_. “Iran’s proposed military budget for 2019 included a 28% cut to its defense budget and a 17% cut to its terror branch, the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps.”
---
|
---
| “For anyone who cares about the common good, this nation desperately needed to hear Trump say what he said Monday,” the [_Washington Examiner_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=b35cdde480&e=3ee4985eea) editorial board writes. Unfortunately, “partisan hackery can transcend any national tragedy . . . Shame on all of them, and on all of the others who want the nation to be upset and fragmented and divided by tragedy, just so that they can sooner get the political outcome they are hoping for.”
---
|
---
| “For the last three years, [the left has] said that attacks on the press are attacks on democracy itself. Yet, many wasted no time this week torching the _New York Times_ for a headline they did not like. They pressured the newspaper into changing its coverage to better suit the Democratic perspective,” Becket Adams writes in the [_Washington Examiner_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=7beb679099&e=3ee4985eea). “The _New York Times_ , of course, caved to peer pressure.”
---
|
---
| “Under President Trump, the U.S. is experiencing record-low unemployment and rising wages the previous administration repeatedly said wasn’t possible. As a result, small businesses are hiring at a record pace,” Job Creators Network CEO Alfredo Ortiz writes for [Fox News](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=0bcb3f20ff&e=3ee4985eea). “President Trump’s goal of energy dominance has played a big part in this economic expansion. Rather than restrict energy production, Trump policies are creating record production of oil and natural gas.”
---
|

[Privacy Policy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=4322a90113&e=3ee4985eea) | [Contact the White House](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=e441967c70&e=3ee4985eea) | [Unsubscribe](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=6cc13034a1)
---

subject: Trump in Dayton and El Paso
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tim Ryan for America
body:

Today, Trump is visiting both Dayton, OH and El Paso, TX in the wake of the mass shootings there. He is being met with protests -- not only by politicians and elected officials -- but by everyday Americans who are fed up with his rhetoric.

I think Trump's visits are a mistake and I stand with these protestors who are holding strong for change and demanding that Trump and his GOP colleagues in the Senate take immediate action to prevent these tragedies from ever happening again.

[**If you 're with us, add your name now to demand Mitch McConnell bring the Senate back into session and take action immediately! »**](http://secure.timryanforamerica.?g=i0wvQ1H8x8Va89sNxWeKKQ "http://secure.timryanforamerica.com/page/s/end-gun-violence?firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=timryan&utm_content=1+-+If+yoursquore+with+us+add+your+name+now+&utm_campaign=TR_EM_RR_ACT_190807_1_1&source=TR_EM_RR_ACT_190807_1_1")

Trump is incapable of showing the level of emotion that is needed to heal the country. Empty words will not stem the tide of violence ripping through our nation.

Thoughts and prayers aren't going to help us. We need real change, real solutions, and real ACTION. I want to see our president and my colleagues in Congress DO SOMETHING.

**Every American that is ready to say "enough is enough" -- I'm with you. I pledge to do everything in my power to move the ball forward on real bipartisan solutions -- including background checks, increased penalties for illegal firearm sales, red flag laws that keep guns out of the hands of dangerous individuals and more.**

Tim

body:
#
Team,
This campaign is about working families, not millionaires. I know and will fight for the interests of working people--because I am a working person. And I will always listen to you, not corporate donors or special interests.
The same can't be said for my opponent, Steve Daines, another ultra-wealthy member of Congress. He and his boss Mitch McConnell are out-of-touch millionaires who are enacting a corrupt, partisan agenda and serving the interests of their fellow wealthy elites.
[**In the Senate, I will fight for working people and work to ensure that billionaires and corporations pay their fair share. Help send me to D.C. with a $5 contribution now.**](https://act.wilmotcollins.?g=ENsuPVNw52hc6i6rfY8XtkA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wc-email?refcode=em190807&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=")
According to a 2014 article from KGVO Talk Radio, Daines' "wealth has increased substantially" while in Congress. "Average last year around $14 million, and then, this year, around $24 million."
And the Center for Responsive Politics reports that Majority Leader Mitch McConnell's net worth increased from $3 million in 2005 to a whopping $27 million in 2015.
I'm not running for office to get rich, team. I'm not going to the Senate to do the bidding of the rich, the well-connected, and the special interests. I'm running for you and all Americans.
Steve Daines and Mitch McConnell are millionaires fighting for billionaires. As your next U.S. Senator, I want to fight for you. But I need your support to help me get to Washington, D.C.
[**Montanans deserve a Senator who will stand with them, not the millionaires. I 'm ready to be that Senator. Will you chip in $5 now to help me defeat Steve Daines in 2020?**](https://act.wilmotcollins.?g=ENsuPVNw52hc6i6rfY8XtkA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wc-email?refcode=em190807&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=")

Wilmot

| |
---
| Elections are about winning - and, unfortunately, losing.
From the beginning, I have prayed, not to win, but to seek and find the Lord's wisdom and plan in everything we did.
While we are disappointed in last night's results, we are satisfied with the race we ran and grateful for the relationships we built along the way.
We are thankful for the thousands of Mississippians who put their faith and trust in us by casting their ballot on the promise of our campaign.
And to those who invested in our grassroots campaign, financially and otherwise, we are eternally grateful.
It is with heavy hearts, but endless optimism for Mississippi's future, that we take time away from public life to spend with family and friends and return to our farm.
We will do our part in this election after we have had time to clear our minds, pray, and discuss with family what's in the best interests of Mississippians.
As we close our campaign for governor and continue to build on our movement, here are two things you can help with:
1\. Continue to Pray for Us Please consider praying for Heather and I as we take that time to pray and seek God's will in our future plans.

clean_body: | | | | | | | | | == | | | Elections are about winning - and, unfortunately, losing. From the beginning, I have prayed, not to win, but to seek and find the Lord's wisdom and plan in everything we did. While we are disappointed in last night's results, we are satisfied with the race we ran and grateful for the relationships we built along the way. We are thankful for the thousands of Mississippians who put their faith and trust in us by casting their ballot on the promise of our campaign. And to those who invested in our grassroots campaign, financially and otherwise, we are eternally grateful. It is with heavy hearts, but endless optimism for Mississippi's future, that we take time away from public life to spend with family and friends and return to our farm. We will do our part in this election after we have had time to clear our minds, pray, and discuss with family what's in the best interests of Mississippians. As we close our campaign for governor and continue to build on our movement, here are two things you can help with: 1\. Continue to Pray for Us Please consider praying for Heather and I as we take that time to pray and seek God's will in our future plans. == We have financial obligations we must meet before we can begin to close our campaign for governor and focus on our message that resonated across America. Any contribution you make will go towards these efforts and help us remain financially sound moving forward. -- == | Again, thank you, Mississippi. name: Francis Conole
subject: No more thoughts and prayers. We must take action.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Conole for Congress
body:
| [](conoleforcongress.com)
---

| | I'm heartbroken and furious.

First it was Gilroy, then El Paso, and then Dayton. Too many communities have been plagued by gun violence and mass shootings.

Enough is enough. We must ban assault weapons to stop this epidemic from endangering our families, children, and community members.

[**I wrote about banning assault weapons in an op-ed for Syracuse.com. Click here to read it now.**](https://conole.bsd.net/page/m/c82b18c/388db0e9/817d902/609afd8b/3872999426/VEsH/?g=G-UoSFH8x8Vc1mgFDtC1r5A "https://www.facebook.com/conole4congress/posts/447286746120178?utm_medium=email&utm_source=conole&utm_content=1+-+I+wrote+about+banning+assault+weapons+in&utm_campaign=FC_EM_EN_90DO_190807_1_1&source=FC_EM_EN_90DO_190807_1_1&refcode=FC_EM_EN_90DO_190807_1_1")

It's infuriating to think 5-year-olds at our Central New York elementary schools, and children all over the country, live in a world where they must prepare and train for an armed assailant situation similar to our soldiers. Our children no longer need our thoughts and prayers. They need and demand our action.

As a Navy veteran, I've trained with all types of guns, but one thing is clear: we need to strengthen gun safety laws and prevent dangerous people from purchasing guns -- starting with criminal background checks on all gun sales and a ban on assault weapons.

The House has passed legislation to prevent gun violence, but Mitch McConnell and Senate Republicans refuse to vote on it.

[**Listen, we must do something to stop this epidemic. Read my op-ed on banning assault weapons here.**](https://conole.bsd.net/page/m/c82b18c/388db0e9/817d902/609afd8a/3872999426/VEsE/?g=G-UoSFH8x8Vc1mgFDtC1r5A "https://www.facebook.com/conole4congress/posts/447286746120178?utm_medium=email&utm_source=conole&utm_content=2+-+FIRSTNAMEListen+we+must+do+something+to+&utm_campaign=FC_EM_EN_90DO_190807_1_1&source=FC_EM_EN_90DO_190807_1_1&refcode=FC_EM_EN_90DO_190807_1_1")

Thank you,

Francis

|
---|---|---

|
---

_This message was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Francis Conole is running to represent New York 's 24th Congressional District to bring fresh leadership to Central NY. A fourth-generation Syracusan and Iraq War veteran, he has dedicated his life to service and sacrifice. You can learn more about his mission by liking us on **[Facebook](https://conole.bsd.net/page/m/c82b18c/388db0e9/817d902/609afd89/3872999426/VEsF/?g=G-UoSFH8x8Vc1mgFDtC1r5A "https://www.facebook.com/conole4congress/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=conole&utm_content=3+-+Facebook&utm_campaign=FC_EM_EN_90DO_190807_1_1&source=FC_EM_EN_90DO_190807_1_1&refcode=FC_EM_EN_90DO_190807_1_1")** and following us on **[Twitter](https://conole.bsd.net/page/m/c82b18c/388db0e9/817d902/609afd88/3872999426/VEsC/?g=G-UoSFH8x8Vc1mgFDtC1r5A "https://twitter.com/Conole4Congress?lang=en&utm_medium=email&utm_source=conole&utm_content=4+-+Twitter&utm_campaign=FC_EM_EN_90DO_190807_1_1&source=FC_EM_EN_90DO_190807_1_1&refcode=FC_EM_EN_90DO_190807_1_1")**. **[Click here to contribute to our campaign.](https://conole.bsd.?g=G-UoSFH8x8Vc1mgFDtC1r5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/fc-footer?refcode=footer&amount=15&recurring=true&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=conole&utm_content=5+-+Click+here+to+contribute+to+our+campaign&utm_campaign=FC_EM_EN_90DO_190807_1_1&source=FC_EM_EN_90DO_190807_1_1")**

We're relying on grassroots supporters like you. But, if you wish to no longer receive emails from us, you can **[unsubscribe here.](https://conole.bsd.net/page/m/c82b18c/388db0e9/817d902/609afd96/3872999426/VEsA/?g=G-UoSFH8x8Vc1mgFDtC1r5A "https://conole.bsd.net/unsubscribe?utm_medium=email&utm_source=conole&utm_content=6+-+unsubscribe+here&utm_campaign=FC_EM_EN_90DO_190807_1_1&source=FC_EM_EN_90DO_190807_1_1&refcode=FC_EM_EN_90DO_190807_1_1")** Use of military rank, job titles, and photographs in uniform does not imply endorsement from the Department of the Navy or the Department of Defense._

name: =?utf-8?q?Jen_O=E2=80=99Malley_Dillon=2C_Beto_for_America?=
subject: Trump’s attacks on Beto
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Beto for America
body:
Donald Trump was up late tweeting cheap shots at Beto from his iPhone. He told El Paso to “be quiet.”

We woke up this morning to find out that Donald Trump was up late tweeting cheap shots at Beto from his iPhone. He told Beto to “be quiet.”
Mr. President, 22 people are dead after an act of terror inspired by your racist words and deeds.
El Paso will not be quiet.
Beto will not be quiet.
This campaign will not be quiet.
We’ll call it as we see it, especially on a day like today.
[If you’re with us, please visit our website to find out more about how you can help El Paso.](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/23763?t=1&akid=47552%2E444211%2E8o1AEH)
**[Find out more »](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/23763?t=2&akid=47552%2E444211%2E8o1AEH)**
This president helped create the hatred that made Saturday's tragedy possible. He should not be coming to El Paso at all. The last thing this town needs is more division. It’s time for healing.
Thank you for being with us during this time.
Jen O’Malley Dillon
Campaign Manager
Beto for America

Paid for by Beto for America
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. This campaign is powered by grassroots supporters like you. But if you'd like to receive fewer emails, [you can do so here](https://act.betoorourke.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/). If you need to remove yourself from our email list, [click here to unsubscribe](https://act.betoorourke.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=3&akid=47552%2E444211%2E8o1AEH). To contribute via check, please address to Beto for America campaign, P.O. Box 3628 El Paso, TX 79923.

Friend -- We’ve been traveling the nation, having real, honest conversations with voters about their concerns about their jobs, their families, border security, and our dangerous foreign-policy-by-tweet. It’s now time for us to showcase what we’ve learned from all of you, all over the country, so we’re demanding a Republican debate with Donald Trump. We could talk about deficits, border failure, character...and what it actually means to be President. If you agree that Americans deserve to hear from both Republican candidates, with no handlers, [sign our petition to challenge Donald Trump to join me on the debate stage.](https://www.weld2020.org/time_to_debate?e=6bbde6f28245738982c2a8d24644af48&utm_source=weld2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=19_08_06_time_to_debate&n=1 "Time to Debate!") We’ve been trying to get a debate on the books for months, but so far our calls for transparency and engagement have been widely dismissed. Local groups tell us that they’ve been ignored by President Trump’s campaign, and it’s becoming even more likely that Donald Trump will refuse to face the tough questions he’s been ducking for years.******Presidential debates are important. Candidates defending records, words, and ideas, without handlers.******[**Bill’s ready. Any time, any place. Sign on to make that a reality >>**](https://www.weld2020.org/time_to_debate?e=6bbde6f28245738982c2a8d24644af48&utm_source=weld2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=19_08_06_time_to_debate&n=2 "Time to Debate!") \-- Team Weld [Contribute](https://www.weld2020.org/donate?utm_campaign=debate_petition&utm_medium=email&utm_source=weld2020&e=6bbde6f28245738982c2a8d24644af48&utm_source=weld2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=19_08_06_time_to_debate&n=3)

### [El Paso and Dayton](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jdlllka-kujkulhluu-j/)
Well, it's happened again. Twice. Crazy/evil men with guns, killing and wounding innocent people, just for the hell of it. This time in El Paso, Texas. And just up the road from our community, in Dayton.

Click the link below to read my thoughts in this week's blog. [Read this entry >>](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jdlllka-kujkulhluu-t/)
# Previous Blog Posts

### [Kowtowing to China](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jdlllka-kujkulhluu-d/)

Last week, the news got consumed by one story - President Trump's ongoing battle with four Democratic Congresswomen. As a result of this media frenzy, there were other important matters that got no coverage whatsoever - that you probably never even heard about. I'd like to tell you about one of those things, that really mattered to a too-often overlooked segment of our country.

name: Tim Ryan
subject: coming together to heal
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tim Ryan for America
body:
|
---
I've done a lot of thinking and reflecting since the events in Dayton and El Paso this weekend. Not just about the tragic loss of life, but about the collective trauma we've all been feeling every single time we turn on the news to another mass shooting.

It's clear that we need to heal, but we can only heal together.

[**Democrats and Republicans. People of all races, creeds and genders. It 's time to come together to find solutions. Click here to add your name to join the call for unity.**](http://secure.timryanforamerica.com/page/m/d7a9db2/5486deaa/3dc57f9b/dc8f53f/2825478462/VEsH/?g=i0wvQ1H8x8Va89sNxWeKKQ "https://secure.timryanforamerica.com/page/s/join-the-call-for-unity?utm_medium=email&utm_source=timryan&utm_content=1+-+Democrats+and+Republicans+People+of+all+&utm_campaign=TR_EM_PT_ACT_190807_1_1&source=TR_EM_PT_ACT_190807_1_1")

We also cannot forget about self-care in times like these. We have to make sure we take care of ourselves as we continue in this fight. Here are a few of the techniques that I use.
* Take a break from TV and social media
* Try Andrew Weil's 4-7-8 breathing/meditation technique
* Workout (physical activity can increase endorphins and make us feel better)
* Hang out with friends and loved ones

Please share and check in on friends and family that might be triggered by recent events and the news.

It's easy to lose sight of one another in our busy world, but now more than ever we need to take the time to come together to heal, find solutions, and take action. Our children, their children, and all future generations are counting on us to fix this now.

[**So please, click here to add your name and join the call for unity.**](http://secure.timryanforamerica.com/page/m/d7a9db2/5486deaa/3dc57f9b/dc8f53e/2825478462/VEsE/?g=i0wvQ1H8x8Va89sNxWeKKQ "https://secure.timryanforamerica.com/page/s/join-the-call-for-unity?utm_medium=email&utm_source=timryan&utm_content=2+-+So+please+click+here+to+add+your+name+an&utm_campaign=TR_EM_PT_ACT_190807_1_1&source=TR_EM_PT_ACT_190807_1_1")

Thanks,

Tim
